title: rFactor (339790)
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: rFactor
- Steam AppID of the game: 339790

## System Information
- GPU: Nvidia GeFoce GTX-1060-6GB
- Driver/LLVM version: 430.50
- Kernel version:5.0
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/leillo1975/d1246bafd9d43aaa2059fd065ee193ea
- Proton version: 4.11-9

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

 
[steam-339790.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/3947246/steam-339790.log)


## Symptoms  
**The game works great if you activate D9VK** (without d9vk the game works at very low framerate, you need to deactivate CSMT). Force Feedback is present  but with some effects missing using [Logitech wheel driver New-Lg4Ff](https://github.com/berarma/new-lg4ff) because the game is affected by the[ force feedback bug of the 4.11-9 proton version](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/wine/pull/70#issuecomment-559812850).

## Reproduction
Simply push start

title: DarkStar One (12330)
text:

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.



[steam-12330.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7799231/steam-12330.log)

## Symptoms 

* During installation the DirectX installer crashes
* During startup, a quick flash to a black screen, after which the game's error reporting dialog appears.

## Reproduction

1. Install the game
2. Try to run it

title: Abiotic Factor (427410)
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Abiotic Factor
- Steam AppID of the game: 427410

## System Information
- GPU: RX 7800XT
- Video driver version: mesa 24.0.6-2
- Kernel version: 6.8.9-arch1-1
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/ivakam/866f3041dadbcbf5d861e8ebb65fcd86
- Proton version: Experimental [bleeding-edge], also tried latest GE and latest stable (9.0-1)

[steam-427410.zip](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/15225421/steam-427410.zip)
## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.
 
## Symptoms 
VOIP settings show no inputs, despite all my audio inputs working for other games, and other VOIP services. The game also doesn't show up under the "Recording" tab in pavucontrol.

This is may be due to UE5's `AudioCapture` plugin lacking a Linux implementation (you can search for the relevant error in the attached log, there's also some info albeit sparse online). I'm not familiar enough with the specifics UE to know whether or not this is the actual issue however, since the game isn't actually running on Linux when using wine/proton.

## Reproduction
Install and run the game on any Linux machine as far as I know. Issue persists regardless of proton version, and is visible from the main menu settings.
